sections.paragraph:
κουστωδία, ας, ἡ (POxy 294, 20 [22 a.d.]; PRyl 189, 2; BGU 341, 3; s. Hahn 233, 6; 234, 7 w. lit.—Lat. loanw., custodia, also in rabb.) a group of soldiers doing guard duty, a guard composed of soldiers Mt 27:66; 28:11. ἔχειν κουστωδίαν take a guard 27:65.—M-M.

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

Hahn Hahn = LH., Rom und Romanismus im griechischen-römischen Osten—List 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Lat. Lat. = Latin

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 563
